name: Fon
notes: Fon (fɔ̀ngbè) — Gbe (Atlantic-Congo) language of Benin. Written in a Latin-based alphabet following the Africa Alphabet, with letters such as ɖ, ɛ, ɔ and digraphs for prenasalised and labialised consonants, plus obligatory tone diacritics (Fon is a tonal language with contrastive high/low and contour tones). GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: a reliable spec must jointly model the segmental letters, nasal vowels and the tone-marking system, which requires dedicated sourcing. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Latin-script Fon orthography with tone diacritics.
